title: Client Onboarding Automation for Restaurants
metaDescription: Done-for-you onboarding sequence and new client setup for restaurants. Every new client, vendor, or catering booking moves through a calm, consistent flow.
excerpt: A done-for-you onboarding sequence for restaurants that walks every new client, vendor, or catering booking through setup without manual chasing or missed steps.

sections.intro:
Restaurants live and die by the next service, which means onboarding new catering clients, private dining bookings, vendors, and franchise partners often gets squeezed into the gaps between shifts. Contracts sit unsigned in someone's inbox. Deposits get chased twice. Allergy notes and headcount changes live in three different threads. By the time the event arrives, the kitchen is working from notes scribbled at 11pm the night before.

Client Onboarding Automation is an AIOS capability that gives every new client a clean, repeatable setup the moment they say yes. The system handles the welcome message, contract, deposit reminder, intake form, and kitchen handoff in your voice, on your schedule, without you babysitting the inbox.
sections.fourCFit:
This page sits in the Capabilities layer of your AI Operating System. Capabilities are the done-for-you workflows that actually do the work once Context and Connections are in place.

  • Context: your menus, pricing, deposit rules, dietary policies, and house voice.
  • Connections: your reservation or CRM system, inbox, calendar, e-sign tool, and payment processor.
  • Capabilities: the onboarding sequence itself, which is what this page covers.
  • Cadence: reminders, nudges, and kitchen handoffs that fire on a schedule without anyone pressing send.
sections.whatDoneLooksLike:
When this capability is installed, a new client booking triggers a complete onboarding flow on its own.

  • Welcome email sent within 60 seconds of booking, in your voice, with next steps and timeline.
  • Contract and deposit invoice drafted, attached, and sent within 10 minutes.
  • Intake form covering headcount, dietary restrictions, service style, and venue notes, sent and tracked.
  • Reminder sequence for unsigned contracts and unpaid deposits at 48 hours, 5 days, and 7 days.
  • Internal handoff brief delivered to the chef and front-of-house lead 7 days and 24 hours before the event.
  • Single source of truth per client, so any team member can see status without asking.
  • SLA: every new booking is fully onboarded with contract sent and intake started within 15 minutes, any hour, any day.
sections.howWeInstall:
1. Audit (free, about 10 minutes). We map your current booking-to-event flow, find where clients fall through, and confirm this capability is the right first install. Run the audit.
  2. Install (10 business days). We document your voice and policies, connect the tools you already use, build the onboarding sequence, and test it end to end with real bookings before going live.
  3. Operate (ongoing). The system runs on its own. We add one new automation per week, tune the language, and report what is working. See plans.
sections.expectedResults:
• Time to first client touch drops from hours or days to under 60 seconds.
  • Contract signing time typically moves from 5 to 9 days down to 1 to 3 days.
  • Deposit collection rate improves by 15 to 30 percent because reminders never get skipped.
  • Manager time spent on onboarding admin drops by 6 to 12 hours per week, depending on volume.
  • Kitchen and front-of-house get consistent, on-time briefs, which reduces day-of surprises.
  • Standing guarantee: you recover 40 or more hours a month across the AIOS, or we keep working free until you do.
sections.faq:
Will this make our restaurant feel robotic to clients?

No. We write the sequence in your voice using your existing language, menus, and tone. Most clients read it as a thoughtful, well-run operation, not automation. You own every word and can edit anything at any time.

We book catering, private dining, and vendor partners in different ways. Can one flow handle that?

Yes. We build branches inside one onboarding capability, so each booking type gets the right contract, intake, and handoff while the underlying system stays simple. You still get one place to see everyone in flight.

Do we have to change our reservation or CRM system?

No. The AIOS is tool-agnostic and connects to what you already run on. If your current setup has a real gap, we will tell you in the audit, but the default is to work with your stack, not replace it.
